key_points: Dorm rooms compared to prison cells with rusty, outdated beds
Kerr Hall mentioned positively; room matching process exists
No standout praise for dorms across any source
key_quotes: If you are arrested your prison cell will bear a striking similar resemblance to a UNT dorm
The beds are shaky and outdated held together by a rusty weld and the prayers that you don't fall through
The mattress will give you scoliosis

key_points: Football atmosphere dramatically improved — first sellouts in school history in 2024
Party scene mixed: frat parties get shut down, Black org/venue parties praised
Annual street preacher visits described as the rare campus unity moment
key_quotes: most schools have their moments of unity at football games, ours was once a year during the street preacher convention lmao
I personally have to disagree with you on the parties... if you attend a venue party them things be LIVE!!!
I only went to one party that I had to pay for - never again

key_points: Campus food overpriced and underwhelming — $7 for a few mozzarella sticks
Chick-fil-A lines notorious even with no wait
Students say skipping campus food and going off-campus is smarter financially
key_quotes: here at union burger we serve you crumbs
this is why i rather fucking starve bro. i can't believe some of the pricing at UNT food court area
Damn for seven bucks you could've gotten really good nachos at fuzzys

key_points: Loneliness and social isolation mentioned by multiple first-year students
Program cuts caused genuine distress — students reported inability to focus on classwork
UNT's clinical mental health grad program nationally recognized and draws students specifically for it
key_quotes: last semester was my first semester at UNT, I feel you on the felt lonely part, trying to figure out how to put myself out there
The university basically said my studies are worthless and I haven't been able to get any significant school work finished since. I'm in shock
UNT definitely want to attend for graduate school because they have the best clinical mental health program in the US

key_points: Free tuition program for Texas residents under $100k income, top 25% class rank or Pell-eligible
International student enrollment drop caused $45 million budget shortfall affecting whole university
Scholarship portal described as confusing and worse than prior year's version
key_quotes: Wish I had this but nonetheless this is amazing
Visual Schedule Builder being gone & this new scholarship portal being straight cheeks compared to last year's
So only first time freshmen? Does that mean transfers won't get it?

key_points: Linguistics and other programs cut without notifying faculty — department chairs blindsided
CS professor Chambers Curtis rated 1.5 on RMP; students switching majors because of him
Strong specific programs: Music, Logistics/Supply Chain, Clinical Mental Health nationally recognized
key_quotes: Yeah he's utter trash, his classes gave me the final push I needed to switch out of comp sci
Linguistics faculty were not notified of the removal of the linguistics degrees until the same email sent to students
UNT has one of the best Logistics and Supply Chain curriculums in the nation

key_points: President Keller lives in Austin, not Denton — skipped fall graduations
Faculty and department chairs excluded from major decisions including degree eliminations
Art exhibit censored without explanation; faculty penned open letter condemning it
key_quotes: The more I hear about Keller, the more I'm glad his signature isn't on my degree
I've been at UNT as Faculty for almost 15 years and have never felt more gross working for UNT
He doesn't live in Denton... That explains why he didn't even show at some of the graduations in the fall

key_points: Campus is genuinely beautiful — photos and campus pride posts get hundreds of upvotes
Parking is a persistent nightmare; students pay for permits but can't find spots
Scooters and pedestrian chaos on sidewalks is a daily hazard
key_quotes: I don't understand why they expect us to pay out the ass for parking but then actually refuse to provide said parking
the people who ride on those scooters do not have pedestrians in mind when they hop on their little bike
there is plenty of parking available, just not in the location that you want

key_points: Specific programs like Logistics/Supply Chain and Music have strong alumni outcomes
Students note UNT's inclusive, non-prestige reputation can be a hiring disadvantage
Having a good football program mentioned as genuinely helping diploma perception
key_quotes: UNT has one of the best Logistics and Supply Chain curriculums in the nation... I learned so much in that degree that prepared me for all facets of business
This sounds dumb but having a good football program is better for your diploma and your future ability to be hired
it's a shame that collegiate prestige is largely determined by exclusion. UNT is not perceived as prestigious because we are inclusive

key_points: Free tuition program for qualifying Texas residents makes UNT exceptional value
Admin bloat at top — president earns $800k, up 16% from predecessor, amid $45M deficit
Program cuts happening while spending on athletics infrastructure continues
key_quotes: Building cathedrals while the flock starves
It's like being broke and jobless, getting a Lamborghini lease
Let's start cutting at the top before the elimination of 70+ UNT academic programs

key_points: Genuinely warm, quirky community — students help strangers, post lost-and-found on Reddit
Football revival in 2024 created historic school spirit and sold-out games for first time
Campus wildlife (possums, hawks, turtles, squirrels) beloved and frequently posted about
key_quotes: Women helping women. WOMEN HELPING WOMEN
I've never been a football fan at all. but this year has been really really cool to see
Yall go to UNT, are yall shocked lol

key_points: **Administration**: President lives in Austin, skipped graduations, cut programs without telling faculty
**Budget Crisis**: $45M deficit triggered 70+ program cuts while admin salaries increased 16%
**Campus Safety**: Administration kept campus open on packed ice, emergency exits iced over, staff snow days eliminated
key_quotes: Linguistics faculty were not notified of the removal of the linguistics degrees until the same email sent to students
There are emergency exits at Sage hall that still have a sheet of ice outside the doors... I slipped and fell Friday myself
Keller also took away staff snow days — staff is now required to work where we used to also get a break. He's a disaster of a leader so far

key_points: UNT Music Library is one of the most famous in the country — and world
Turtle pond at the Environmental Education Building is a beloved, low-key campus spot
Strong student community on Reddit actively helps each other with schedules, lost items, and campus life
key_quotes: UNT music library is one of the most famous in the country if not the world. We go to a prestigious school
they rlly love apples! they were jumping on top of each other when i was feeding them some lol
If you feed three of the turtles a Cheez-It, they give you a wish
